# First check what changed from last action
if last_transition:
    before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']
    after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation['nodes']
    before_map = {n['id']: n for n in before}
    after_map = {n['id']: n for n in after}
    print("Before last action:")
    for n in before:
        if n['color'] in ('N', 'Y', 'G', 'r') and n['pixels'] <= 20:
            print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
    print("After last action:")
    for n in after:
        if n['color'] in ('N', 'Y', 'G', 'r') and n['pixels'] <= 20:
            print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
    
    # Find what changed
    for nid in set(list(before_map.keys()) + list(after_map.keys())):
        bn = before_map.get(nid)
        an = after_map.get(nid)
        if bn and an:
            if bn['pixels'] != an['pixels'] or bn['boundary'] != an['boundary']:
                print(f"\nChanged id={nid}: before pixels={bn['pixels']} boundary={bn['boundary']}, after pixels={an['pixels']} boundary={an['boundary']}")
        elif bn:

</function>
</tool_call>

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on yellow shape id=20
actions = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 38, 'col': 24}]
result = action(actions)
print(f"Result: {result}")

# Check what changed
if last_transition:
    before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']
    after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation['nodes']
    before_map = {n['id']: n for n in before}
    after_map = {n['id']: n for n in after}
    
    # Check bars
    for n in after:
        if n['color'] in ('N', 'Y') and n['pixels'] <= 20:
            print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
    
    # Find gameplay changes (ignore timer bar at row 63)
    for nid in set(list(before_map.keys()) + list(after_map.keys())):
        bn = before_map.get(nid)
        an = after_map.get(nid)
        if bn and an:
            # Skip timer bar
            if any(p[0] == 63 for p in bn.get('boundary', [])):
                continue
            if bn['pixels'] != an['pixels'] or bn['boundary'] != an['boundary']:
                print(f"Changed id={nid}: before pixels={bn['pixels']} boundary={bn['boundary']}, after pixels={an['pixels']} boundary={an['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
